terms.definition: Surgical removal of the ovaries and uterus in a female animal; the standard 'spay' procedure.
terms.exampleSentence: The bitch is booked in for an ovariohysterectomy on Tuesday morning.
terms.contextNotes: Often abbreviated 'OVH' or 'spay' in clinical notes.

terms.definition: Surgical removal of the testes in a male animal to prevent reproduction and reduce androgen-driven behaviour.
terms.exampleSentence: Routine castration is recommended at six to nine months of age in this breed.
terms.contextNotes: Owners often use 'neutering' colloquially for both sexes.

terms.definition: Mechanical removal of plaque and calculus from teeth, typically performed under general anaesthesia in dogs and cats.
terms.exampleSentence: She is scheduled for dental scaling and polishing under GA next week.
terms.contextNotes: GA is essential; awake scaling does not address subgingival calculus.

terms.definition: Administration of anthelmintic medication to eliminate intestinal parasitic worms in an animal.
terms.exampleSentence: Routine deworming every three months is advised for households with young children.
terms.contextNotes: Schedule depends on lifestyle and exposure risk.

terms.definition: Subcutaneous insertion of a permanent radio-frequency identification chip for animal identification.
terms.exampleSentence: Microchipping is a legal requirement for all dogs over eight weeks of age in the UK.
terms.contextNotes: Owners must update keeper details when they move address.

terms.definition: Acquisition of diagnostic X-ray images while the animal is chemically restrained but not under full anaesthesia.
terms.exampleSentence: We will perform thoracic radiography under sedation to minimise stress.
terms.contextNotes: Used when GA risk outweighs benefit or for short studies.

terms.definition: Topical or systemic ectoparasiticide applied to control or prevent flea infestation on a companion animal.
terms.exampleSentence: Apply the spot-on flea treatment to the skin between the shoulder blades.
terms.contextNotes: Treat all in-contact animals and the environment together.

terms.definition: A defined schedule of core and non-core vaccines administered at age-appropriate intervals to confer immunity.
terms.exampleSentence: Please follow the WSAVA vaccination protocol for puppies in the practice manual.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish core (DHPPi, FeLV-status dependent) from non-core vaccines.

terms.definition: The deliberate, humane ending of an animal's life by injection of an overdose of barbiturate to relieve suffering.
terms.exampleSentence: After discussion with the family, we proceeded with euthanasia using intravenous pentobarbital.
terms.contextNotes: Always obtain written, informed consent before proceeding.

terms.definition: Placement of an endotracheal tube into the trachea to secure the airway during general anaesthesia.
terms.exampleSentence: Following induction, smooth intubation was achieved with a size 7 endotracheal tube.
terms.contextNotes: Cats are prone to laryngospasm; topical lidocaine is often used.

terms.definition: A non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) commonly used in veterinary patients for pain and inflammation.
terms.exampleSentence: Continue meloxicam 0.1 mg/kg orally once daily for five days post-operatively.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in dehydration, renal compromise, or concurrent corticosteroid use.

terms.definition: A propionic-acid class NSAID licensed for canine post-operative and chronic musculoskeletal pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Dispense carprofen 4 mg/kg once daily and review liver enzymes at two weeks.
terms.contextNotes: Idiosyncratic hepatopathy is rare but reported in Labradors.

terms.definition: A combination broad-spectrum antibiotic of amoxicillin with the beta-lactamase inhibitor clavulanic acid.
terms.exampleSentence: Start amoxicillin-clavulanate 12.5 mg/kg orally twice daily pending culture results.
terms.contextNotes: First-line choice for many pyodermas and UTIs in small animals.

terms.definition: A third-generation fluoroquinolone antibiotic licensed for dogs and cats with broad Gram-negative activity.
terms.exampleSentence: Marbofloxacin was selected based on culture sensitivity for the resistant urinary infection.
terms.contextNotes: Reserve under stewardship; avoid in growing large-breed dogs (cartilage risk).

terms.definition: A potent alpha-2 adrenergic agonist used as a sedative and analgesic, often combined with an opioid for pre-medication.
terms.exampleSentence: Pre-medicate with medetomidine and butorphanol intramuscularly fifteen minutes before induction.
terms.contextNotes: Reverse with atipamezole if recovery is prolonged.

terms.definition: A synthetic kappa-opioid agonist providing mild to moderate analgesia and sedation in small animals.
terms.exampleSentence: Butorphanol was administered for short-acting analgesia during the dressing change.
terms.contextNotes: Short duration; not adequate for moderate-to-severe surgical pain alone.

terms.definition: A dissociative anaesthetic agent used in combination protocols for induction or as part of analgesic infusions.
terms.exampleSentence: Induction was achieved with a ketamine and midazolam co-induction.
terms.contextNotes: Controlled drug; record use in the controlled drugs register.

terms.definition: A porcine lente insulin preparation licensed for the management of diabetes mellitus in dogs and cats.
terms.exampleSentence: Start Caninsulin at 0.5 IU/kg subcutaneously twice daily and recheck a glucose curve in seven days.
terms.contextNotes: Shake gently before drawing up; resuspends as a cloudy suspension.

terms.definition: A protamine zinc recombinant human insulin licensed primarily for diabetic cats with prolonged duration of action.
terms.exampleSentence: ProZinc 1 IU per cat subcutaneously every twelve hours was prescribed at diagnosis.
terms.contextNotes: Prefer in cats; combine with low-carbohydrate diet.

terms.definition: A volatile halogenated ether maintenance anaesthetic delivered with oxygen via a calibrated vaporiser.
terms.exampleSentence: Anaesthesia was maintained on isoflurane in oxygen at one to two per cent.
terms.contextNotes: Scavenge waste gases to protect theatre personnel.

terms.definition: A short-acting intravenous induction anaesthetic providing rapid loss of consciousness with smooth recovery.
terms.exampleSentence: Induction was performed with propofol given slowly to effect via the cephalic catheter.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid repeated dosing in cats due to oxidative injury risk.

terms.definition: A neuroactive steroid intravenous induction agent licensed for dogs and cats with a wide safety margin.
terms.exampleSentence: Alfaxalone was chosen for induction given the patient's borderline cardiac function.
terms.contextNotes: Titrate to effect; rapid bolus may cause apnoea.

terms.definition: An alpha-2 adrenergic antagonist used to reverse the sedative and cardiovascular effects of medetomidine.
terms.exampleSentence: Atipamezole was administered intramuscularly to reverse the medetomidine.
terms.contextNotes: Volume usually equals the medetomidine dose; never give intravenously.

terms.definition: A partial mu-opioid agonist providing moderate analgesia of relatively long duration in dogs and cats.
terms.exampleSentence: Buprenorphine was given by oral transmucosal route for analgesia in the cat.
terms.contextNotes: Useful in cats due to oral transmucosal absorption.

terms.definition: A short-acting benzodiazepine used for sedation, co-induction, or seizure control in veterinary patients.
terms.exampleSentence: Midazolam was added to the induction protocol to reduce the propofol requirement.
terms.contextNotes: Excitement may occur in healthy patients given alone.
